title: Mega Feraligatr Battle Guide
summary: A [water]/[dragon] physical breaker whose [dragonize] ability turns [return] into a boosted [dragon] STAB on a 160 Attack frame.
strengths: 160 base Attack produces game-ending damage output
weaknesses: Gains a 2x Fairy weakness from the [dragon] type
Turn one still runs the base ability pre-mega

recommendedMoves.reason: [dragonize] converts this into a boosted [dragon] STAB

recommendedItems.reason: Required mega stone for the Feraligatr mega form

bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
Mega Feraligatr evolves into a [water]/[dragon] physical breaker with the [dragonize] ability, which converts all Normal-type moves into [dragon] STAB with a 1.2x damage multiplier. 160 Attack paired with 125 Defense gives the mega form elite physical stats on both sides, and a single [dragon-dance] turns it into a sweeper that clears out most walls in the format. The role is a late-game physical breaker with enough setup potential to close games.

## Strengths
The [dragonize] mechanic is the centerpiece. [return]'s 102 base power multiplied by STAB and the 1.2x ability boost produces effective damage that competes with the strongest single-target physical moves in the game, and 160 Attack puts those numbers into one-shot range against anything neutral. The 125/95 defensive line also means Mega Feraligatr survives setup turns that would break most other sweepers. [dragon]-type coverage also opens up angles against the [dragon] walls the base form can't touch.

## Weaknesses & Answers
The new Fairy weakness is the structural cost of the [dragon] type. 78 Speed is still the pre-dance base, so any faster attacker gets the first move. The pre-mega turn runs the original ability (sheer-force or torrent), which changes how the first attack plays out compared to post-mega numbers.

## Recommended Moveset
[return] is the locked main attack because [dragonize] upgrades it into a boosted [dragon] STAB. [waterfall] is the secondary [water] STAB for targets that resist [dragon]. [earthquake] covers Steel, Electric, and Fairy walls that would wall the STAB combo. [dragon-dance] fills the fourth slot for the speed tier fix and the extra Attack boost. ice-punch is an alternate coverage pick if the team needs more specific [dragon] and Ground coverage.

## Item · Ability · Tera
The item is always [feraligatrite]. The ability is locked to [dragonize] after mega evolving. Tera [dragon] maxes [return] damage further, while Tera [water] flips the Fairy weakness concern and boosts [waterfall] damage simultaneously. Tera choice depends on whether the team wants maximum offense or defensive fallback.

## Teammates & Synergy
A Fairy answer is mandatory. Speed control via tailwind or screens makes the pre-dance turns safer, and a fast pivot creates the entry windows Mega Feraligatr needs to set up. A dedicated hazard remover preserves the bulk on repeated entries, and a physical wall breaker on the back line picks up any Pokemon Mega Feraligatr can't break.

## Play Patterns
Bring Mega Feraligatr in on a predicted switch, mega evolve, dance on the swap turn, then start attacking with the right move per target. [return] against neutral walls, [waterfall] against Steel and Rock, [earthquake] against Electric and Fairy, [dragon-dance] again if the opportunity exists. Save the Tera for the exact moment a Fairy move is telegraphed.
